terms.definition: Scan a collection element by element until the target is found or the end is reached; O(n) on a list of n items.
On a sorted sequence, repeatedly compare against the middle element and discard the half that cannot contain the target; O(log n).
A range `[lo, hi)` that includes `lo` but excludes `hi`; the standard convention for binary search to minimize off-by-one errors.
A yes/no function whose answer flips at most once as the parameter grows; required for 'binary search the answer'.
Generalization of binary search: instead of searching an array, search the value space for the smallest (or largest) parameter satisfying a monotonic predicate.
A related O(n) technique for sorted sequences where two indices move from opposite ends or in lock-step; not binary, but often used alongside it.
